title: Black Coffee on Cotton
stainChemistry: Black coffee (no cream, no sugar) is a single-component tannin stain — chlorogenic acid and phenolic polyphenols dissolved in water. No protein, no oil to complicate the sequence. Anthocyanin-like bonds form through hydrogen bonding between the coffee polyphenols and cotton cellulose. Fresh black coffee rinses out easily; aged coffee oxidizes slowly into a browner, more resistant form. Cotton is pure cellulose — tolerates acid-side tannin spotters, 3% H₂O₂, and hot post-chemistry wash.
whyThisWorks: Without milk protein in the way, black coffee is a straightforward tannin job: cold flush to move what's loose, NSD to emulsify bound pigment, acid-side tannin spotter to disrupt the hydrogen bond, 3% H₂O₂ to oxidize the chromophore, hot wash to finish. Simpler than coffee-with-cream because there's no casein layer that has to be digested first.
defaultAssumption: Black coffee on cotton is the easiest coffee variant. Fresh clears in minutes; aged (overnight+) adds a peroxide or tannin-spotter cycle but rarely escalates beyond home treatment unless heat-set.

spottingProtocol.equipment: Cold running water, white cotton terry towel
spottingProtocol.instruction: Cold flush from the BACK of the fabric. Blot from underneath. On fresh black coffee (<15 min), 80%+ of the stain lifts here. Cold only — hot water on aged coffee sets the tannin brown.
Apply NSD to the stain, sponge gently. NSD suspends residual pigment so the next steps act on what's bound to the fiber. Blot with a clean towel. Should see noticeable lightening.

spottingProtocol.equipment: Kettle of hot — not boiling — water
spottingProtocol.instruction: Only for stains under 30 minutes old. Stretch the fabric taut over a bowl, pour hot water through from the back. Heat breaks hydrogen bonds on fresh tannin. Skip on aged — heat sets older tannin permanently.

spottingProtocol.equipment: Professional tannin spotter (Chemspec Tannin Spotter or PRENETT-A) — acid-side tannin treatment
spottingProtocol.instruction: If any brown shadow remains, apply acid-side tannin spotter by dropper. Disrupts the polyphenol-cellulose hydrogen bond. Dwell 2–5 min, blot. Repeat once if shadow persists.
For residual chromophore: 3% H₂O₂ drop-by-drop. Oxidizes the conjugated polyphenol to colorless. Dwell 3–5 min, blot. Repeat once if needed. One oxidizer at a time, NSD-flushed between.
Flush with NSD until rinse runs clear. Launder hot per care label.

steps: 1. Cold flush from the back — fresh black coffee mostly lifts here.
2. NSD in cold water. Sponge, dwell 2–3 min, blot.
3. FRESH ONLY (<30 min): hot pour-through. Skip on aged.
4. Acid-side tannin spotter (Chemspec Tannin Spotter / PRENETT-A) — dropper, 2–5 min, blot.
5. 3% H₂O₂ on residual. 3–5 min. One pass, NSD-flush between repeats.
6. NSD flush, hot wash per care label.
products: Professional tannin spotter (Chemspec Tannin Spotter or PRENETT-A)
professionalProtocol.temperature: cold through spotting; hot wash post-chemistry
warnings: Never use hot water on aged black coffee — sets tannin permanently
One oxidizer at a time — NSD-flushed between
Flush all chemistry before heat — residual oxidizer yellows cotton

homeSolutions.instruction: Black coffee on cotton is easy to beat if you move fast. Cold water only — hot water sets the tannin. Most fresh spills lift with the next step alone.

homeSolutions.agent: Flush From the Back of the Fabric
homeSolutions.instruction: Hold the BACK of the stained area under cold running water for 30–60 seconds. The coffee pushes out the way it came in. Blot with a clean white towel from underneath — don't rub.

If the stain is under 30 minutes old: stretch the fabric taut over a bowl, place a clean towel underneath, pour hot (not boiling) water through from the back. You'll see the coffee fade dramatically. Skip this if the coffee's been sitting longer than 30 minutes — heat sets older tannin.

homeSolutions.agent: Dawn + White Vinegar for Stubborn Shadow
homeSolutions.instruction: If any brown shadow remains, mix 1 teaspoon Dawn dish soap, 1 tablespoon white vinegar, and 1 cup cold water. Dab onto the stain with a clean cloth, let sit 3 minutes, blot from outside toward center. Repeat once if color persists.

homeSolutions.agent: OxiClean or 3% Hydrogen Peroxide for Lingering Color
homeSolutions.instruction: For stubborn shadows: dissolve 1 scoop OxiClean in 1 cup cool water, soak the stain 15–30 minutes. OR dab 3% drugstore hydrogen peroxide on the spot and let it bubble 3–5 min, blot. Skip on dark or dyed cotton.
homeSolutions.safetyNote: Never mix OxiClean with chlorine bleach, ammonia, or hydrogen peroxide at the same time — one oxidizer at a time.

homeSolutions.agent: Hot Wash, Inspect Before the Dryer
homeSolutions.instruction: Launder on the hottest setting the care label allows. Inspect BEFORE the dryer — dryer heat sets any residual tannin permanently.

If the coffee has already been through a hot dryer or two home rounds haven't cleared it, take it to a dry cleaner. Tell them: "Black coffee on cotton, age [fresh / overnight / older], home-treated with Dawn + vinegar / OxiClean / 3% hydrogen peroxide."

steps: 1. Move fast — cold water only while the stain is visible.
2. Flush cold from the BACK of the fabric, blot from underneath.
4. Dawn + vinegar + cold water solution, 3 min dwell, blot.
5. OxiClean soak OR 3% peroxide on residual shadow. Never layer oxidizers.
6. Hot wash per care label. Inspect BEFORE the dryer.
7. Escalate if heat-set or two rounds failed.
diyProtocol.whenToStop: Stop if black coffee has been through a hot dryer, if two home rounds don't clear it, or if the cotton is dark dyed where peroxide isn't safe.
warnings: Hot water only for fresh — sets aged tannin
Never mix OxiClean with chlorine bleach, ammonia, or hydrogen peroxide
Skip peroxide on dark/dyed cotton — test inside seam first

neverDo: Never use hot water on aged black coffee — sets tannin brown
Never layer oxidizers — one at a time
Never dry with residual chemistry in the fiber
fiberSensitivities: Cotton tolerates NSD, acid-side tannin spotters, 3% H₂O₂, hot wash post-chemistry
Heat-safe fiber once chemistry is cleared

materialWarnings: Hot water only for fresh — sets aged tannin
Peroxide on whites; test inside seam on dark cotton

escalation.when: Heat-set by dryer or iron, two home rounds failed, or dark dyed cotton where peroxide isn't safe.
escalation.whatToTell: "Black coffee on cotton, age [fresh / overnight / older], home-treated with Dawn + vinegar / OxiClean / 3% H₂O₂. [Washed hot / not yet laundered.]"

contextQuestions: Is the cotton white, light, or dark/dyed?
Has it been through a hot wash or dryer since?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: Black coffee is the simplest coffee variant — no protein, no oil. Fresh = trivial DIY. Heat-set or dyed cotton narrows options.
